definition: The path factor of rightly directed attitude or resolve shaping intention away from harm and attachment.
notes: The project prefers right attitude because it captures the ongoing directional quality of the factor better than a single interior thought. Right resolve and right intention remain acceptable when a passage emphasizes deliberate orientation more explicitly. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji uses harmonious orientation in his eightfold-way teaching materials; the repository records that as a source-specific pedagogical alternate.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing deliberate direction
context_rules.notes: Useful when the volitional aspect needs more weight.

context_rules.context: When the passage focuses narrowly on intended aim
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate if attitude would sound too broad.

context_rules.context: In Punnaji-style pedagogical exposition of the eightfold path
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.

example_phrases.notes: Shows one concrete mode of the factor.
